error_handling.overview: Error scenarios the QA mode may encounter and how to handle them. QA never aborts — it always delivers a report, even if degraded.

scenario.name: file_not_found
scenario.description: One or more files listed in the delegator's brief do not exist or cannot be read

recovery.step: Note each missing file with its path in a "File Access Issues" preamble at the top of the report
Continue reviewing the remaining accessible files
Do not abort the review — a partial review is better than no review

scenario.report_example: ## File Access Issues - `src/components/MissingComponent.tsx` — file not found at this path

scenario.name: brief_missing_context
scenario.description: The delegator's brief is too vague — missing the goal, file list, or constraints

recovery.step: If the file list is missing: report "No file list provided — cannot proceed with review." and complete

If the goal is missing but files are provided: review files for general code quality issues. Note in the report: "Goal was not specified — review is based on general code quality, not alignment with intended behavior."
If constraints are missing: proceed without them. Note in the report preamble what was missing.

scenario.name: very_large_file
scenario.description: A file is too large to read in its entirety within reasonable context limits

recovery.step: Read the file in sections using offset/limit
Focus on the parts most likely to contain the changes (delegator should indicate changed sections, but if not, use heuristics: recently modified functions, exported symbols)
Note in the report: "File X was partially reviewed due to size. Focused on [specific sections]."

scenario.name: ambiguous_code
scenario.description: Code is unclear and QA cannot determine whether it's correct or buggy

recovery.step: Report it as a Concern: "The logic in [file:line] is ambiguous — [describe why]. Cannot determine correctness without clarification."
Suggest what clarification or documentation would resolve the ambiguity

scenario.name: no_changes_detected
scenario.description: All files look like existing code — QA can't identify what was changed

recovery.step: Review the files as-is for general code quality, but note in the report: "Unable to identify specific changes — review is of the files in their entirety, not a diff. Some findings may relate to pre-existing code."

scenario.name: conflicting_instructions
scenario.description: The brief contains contradictory constraints or goals

recovery.step: Note the contradiction in the report preamble
Evaluate the code against each constraint separately and report findings for both interpretations
Flag the contradiction as a finding in Concerns & Risks
